---
title: "Exploring Storms"
author: "Avinesh Saravana"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
```

## Storms Per Year by Type

This plot shows the annual count of tropical depressions, tropical storms, and hurricanes recorded in the NOAA Atlantic hurricane database (`dplyr::storms` dataset).

```{r}
#| label: plot-storms
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Calculate the count of each storm type per year
storms_count <- storms %>%
  filter(status %in% c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane")) %>%
  mutate(status = factor(status, levels = c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane"))) %>%
  distinct(year, name, status) %>%
  count(year, status, name = "num_storms") %>%
  complete(year, status, fill = list(num_storms = 0))

# Plot using ggplot2
ggplot(storms_count, aes(x = year, y = num_storms, fill = status)) +
  geom_area(position = "stack", alpha = 0.85, color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "tropical depression" = "#9ecae1",  # Light blue
      "tropical storm"      = "#fdae61",  # Orange
      "hurricane"           = "#d73027"   # Dark red
    ),
    breaks = c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane"),
    labels = c("Tropical Depression", "Tropical Storm", "Hurricane")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual Count of Atlantic Storms by Type",
    subtitle = "Tracking unique storms per year by status (1975 - present)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Storms",
    fill = "Storm Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18, color = "#2c3e50"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "#7f8c8d", margin = margin(b = 15)),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```
